title: Hypothermia / Cold Exposure — Adult First Aid Protocol

source.document_title: MDA Training Department Manual — Hypothermia and Cold Exposure First Aid

import_metadata.notes: Unified from MDA and Hatzalah sources. Hatzalah (United Hatzalah and Hatzalah Without Borders) do not publish a standalone hypothermia protocol; MDA is the sole source. Additional references: Infomed.co.il, Medico.co.il (2026 update). MDA publication date approximated as 2024 based on Emergency Medicine Manual edition.

protocol.summary: First aid for hypothermia (body temperature below 35 degrees C) through gradual rewarming, starting with the torso.
protocol.when_to_apply: Person shows signs of hypothermia: shivering, confusion, pale/cold skin, fatigue, slurred speech, muscle rigidity, or loss of consciousness after cold exposure. Body temperature below 35 degrees C (95 degrees F).

call_emergency.when: Immediately upon recognising hypothermia — call MDA at 101 as the first step.

steps.detail: Hypothermia can deteriorate rapidly. Request ambulance assistance immediately.

steps.instruction: Move the person to a warm, dry shelter.
steps.detail: Protect from wind, rain, and further cold exposure. If outdoors, shield from the elements as best as possible.

steps.instruction: Remove wet clothing promptly and replace with dry layers.
steps.detail: Wet clothing accelerates heat loss. Replace with dry, lightweight layered clothing that allows some movement, as movement generates heat.

steps.instruction: Cover the person with blankets and layers, including the head.
steps.detail: Significant heat is lost through the head. Wrap thoroughly in blankets.

steps.instruction: Warm the room to approximately 22 degrees C (72 degrees F) if indoors.
steps.detail: A warm ambient environment supports gradual rewarming.

steps.instruction: Assess whether the person is conscious.

decision_point.condition: Is the person conscious and able to swallow?
decision_point.if_yes: Provide warm (NOT hot) beverages. Avoid alcohol and caffeine.
decision_point.if_no: Do not give any fluids. Monitor breathing closely and be prepared to begin CPR.

steps.instruction: Apply heat packs to the chest, abdomen, and back ONLY.
steps.detail: Use a cloth barrier between heat packs and skin — never place directly on bare skin. Warm the torso FIRST, not the hands and feet.
steps.warning: Warming extremities first can cause cold blood to rush to the heart, potentially triggering cardiac arrest (afterdrop phenomenon).

decision_point.if_no: Continue monitoring and gradual rewarming until MDA arrives.

steps.instruction: Continue gradual rewarming until emergency services arrive.
steps.detail: Rewarming must be gradual. Rapid rewarming can cause dangerous cardiac arrhythmias.

do_not: Do NOT rub extremities — friction can cause tissue damage and cardiac complications.
Do NOT use direct intense heat (hot water baths, heating pads directly on skin) — rapid rewarming can cause dangerous cardiac arrhythmias.
Do NOT give alcohol — it dilates blood vessels and accelerates heat loss.
Do NOT give caffeine — it worsens hypothermia.
Do NOT warm extremities first — cold blood rushing to the heart can trigger cardiac arrest (afterdrop).
Do NOT apply vigorous external rewarming.
equipment_needed: Warm (not hot) beverages (for conscious patients only)
